message:
feat(auth): add duplicate live session detection with audit trail and reorder validation checks

Previously, if a client reused a session ID that was still in an
Establishing, Active, or Terminating state, the session manager would
silently overwrite the existing session entry. This could mask bugs in
client reconnect logic and created a window where the old session's
replay windows were orphaned without cleanup.

Changes:
- Add ERR_SCC_DUPLICATE_SESSION error code and
  SessionError::DuplicateLiveSession variant with Display impl
- Insert duplicate-session check before max-sessions check, so a reused
  live session ID is rejected with a specific error rather than a
  misleading "max sessions reached" when the limit is near capacity
- Move the max-sessions check to after MAC verification, so an
  unauthenticated client cannot probe session capacity
- Emit SCC_MESSAGE_REJECTED audit event on duplicate session detection
  with "duplicate live session id" detail for forensic tracing
- Clean up stale replay_windows entries for the session ID before
  inserting the new session, preventing unbounded memory growth when
  sessions are rapidly created and destroyed
- Add tests covering DuplicateLiveSession error code mapping and
  renumber subsequent error code assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
